semanticCore: Collecting, choosing, water pool, or tax
quranicRelevance: Collecting fruits/wealth (Jubiyat - 'yujba ilayhi thamaratu kulli shay'), and choosing/selecting prophets (Ijtaba - 'thumma ijtabahu rabbuhu').

keyDerivations.meaning: To collect, gather, bring together, or hoard
To choose, select, pick out, or elect (for favor/prophethood)
Water pools, large basins, or cisterns

figurativeOrRareUsages: يُجْبَى إِلَيْهِ ثَمَرَاتُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ (24:57): To which are gathered/collected the fruits of all things (security of Mecca).
وَقُدُورٍ رَاسِيَاتٍ وَجِفَانٍ كَالجَوَابِي: And heavy cooking pots and bowls like basins/pools.
discardedNoise: Tax collection offices (Ghibayah) in modern fiscal law.
summaryEn: The root denotes collection and selection, describing the gathering of fruits to Mecca, the election (Ijtaba) of prophets, and massive basins (Jawabi).
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الجباية والاصطفاء، واصفاً جلب الأرزاق إلى مكة (يُجبى)، واصطفاء واجتباء الرسل (اجتبى)، وحياض المياه الضخمة (جوابي).
rawText: جبى 1 , (S, Msb, TA,) aor. ﹹ, (Msb, TA,) inf. n. جَثٌّ, (A, K,) He pulled it up, or out; as also 🡻اجتثّهُ; (S, Msb;) or the latter denotes a quicker action than the former; and properly signifies he took its whole جُثَّة [or body]: (TA:) or the former, he cut it; or cut it off: (A, L, K:) or he cut it off from its root: (L:) or he pulled it up, or out, by the root; namely, a tree: (A, K;) he uprooted it, or eradicated it. (A.) ___ جَثَّ said of a collector of honey, He took the honey with its جَثّ and its مَحَارِين, i. e., the bees that had died in it. (IAar, TA.)
